_README: PROJECT ADAPTER. This is the ONLY file you must fill in per project. The generic agents/hooks read it. Leave a value as an empty string to mark a gate 'not configured' (it will be skipped, not failed). See docs/GETTING_STARTED.md and docs/PROMPTS.md.

modules.name: contracts
modules.description: Foundry project: sample interconnected Solidity contracts used as deployment fixtures. Solidity 0.8.28.

modules.name: core
modules.description: @redeploy/core — deployment engine over Hardhat Ignition: declarative spec, dependency resolution/ordering, idempotent journal-based resume. Base package; introduce no cycles.

modules.name: config
modules.description: @redeploy/config — post-deployment configuration: declarative steps, resumable partial configuration, config-state idempotency. Builds on core.

modules.name: verify
modules.description: @redeploy/verify — source/bytecode verification (Etherscan/Sourcify) + on-chain assertion that live configuration matches the declared spec.

modules.name: reader
modules.description: @redeploy/reader — read-only library exposing deployment + config state via a typed API to external systems. Builds on core.

modules.name: studio
modules.description: @redeploy/studio — visual tool (React + React Flow): drag-and-drop authoring of connections/config (emits spec files) + deployment inspector (reads via reader).

modules.name: deploy-server
modules.description: @redeploy/deploy-server — HTTP server exposing deployment simulation and execution as an API (node:http, no framework). GET /health now; POST /api/simulate (SSE) in a follow-up.

modules.name: website
modules.description: @redeploy/website — public marketing site (Vite static build): sells reDeploy's benefits and showcases features with Studio screenshots. No backend.

gates._note: Exact shell commands for this repo. Run from repo root. Empty string = skip. TS via pnpm/vitest, Solidity via forge. install also fetches the (gitignored) Foundry deps idempotently so fresh clones, worktree workers, and CI can run the forge gates without a manual `forge install`. test_affected currently runs the full suite (pnpm has no cheap affected filter here); refine later. TS lint (eslint) and slither are not wired yet — see the tooling ticket.
gates.install: pnpm install && (cd contracts && { [ -d lib/forge-std ] || forge install --no-git foundry-rs/forge-std@v1.16.1; } && { [ -d lib/openzeppelin-contracts ] || forge install --no-git OpenZeppelin/openzeppelin-contracts@v5.1.0; })
gates.lint: pnpm -r lint && pnpm exec solhint 'contracts/src/**/*.sol' 'contracts/test/**/*.sol' && (cd contracts && forge fmt --check)
gates.test: pnpm -r build && pnpm -r test && (cd contracts && forge test)
gates.test_affected: pnpm -r build && pnpm -r test && (cd contracts && forge test)
gates.e2e: pnpm -F @redeploy/core build && (cd contracts && forge build) && pnpm -F @redeploy/core test:e2e

review._consensus_note: 'all' = every lens must approve; or an integer = that many approvals required. Solidity work leans hard on the security lens (reentrancy/access/overflow).

budget._note: Opus to coordinate/review, Sonnet to build, Haiku to explore. Start at 2 parallel workers; raise only when review+merge keeps up.

humanTest._note: Consumed by .claude/scripts/prepare-pr.sh (the /test-pr command). 'prepare' runs INSIDE the PR worktree to make it runnable; 'launch' is printed for the user to run in their own terminal. The studio (apps/studio) is the human-test surface; its Vite dev server proxies /api to the deploy-server on :8787 (see apps/studio/vite.config.ts), so BOTH must run. prepare builds the libs the studio imports (core/config/reader) AND the deploy-server (its dev script runs a prebuilt dist/index.js). launch runs both in parallel; Ctrl-C stops both. prepare-pr.sh also symlinks the repo-root .env into the worktree for the server. launchPhone (opt-in, via `/test-pr <n> --phone`) additionally exposes the already-running studio to a phone through a cloudflared quick tunnel (--http-host-header localhost makes cloudflared send Host: localhost so Vite's allowedHosts guard accepts it — no app config change). It backgrounds the dev servers and runs cloudflared in the foreground so its printed https://<random>.trycloudflare.com URL is visible; Ctrl-C tears down both the tunnel and the dev servers. It is PUBLIC, ephemeral, and exposes /api (which can trigger real deploys) — attended use only.
humanTest.launchPhone: sh -c 'pnpm -r --parallel --filter @redeploy/deploy-server --filter @redeploy/studio dev & devpid=$!; trap "kill $devpid 2>/dev/null" EXIT INT TERM; echo "waiting for studio on :5173 …"; cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:5173 --http-host-header localhost'
